_attribution: LA FORJA team (Manuel Peña Zúñiga). Original item authored by the team.
stem: One card is drawn at random from a standard 52-card deck. What is the probability that the card drawn is an ace or a club?
author_rationale: There are 4 aces and 13 clubs, but the ace of clubs belongs to both sets: 4 + 13 - 1 = 16 favorable cases, so P = 16/52 = 4/13. Each distractor matches a real error: 17/52 forgets inclusion-exclusion and simply adds 4 + 13, 1/4 = 13/52 counts only the clubs, and 1/13 = 4/52 counts only the aces.
